text.title: Streaming and Async Parallel Requests
text.summary: Streaming with stream=True for real-time output; AsyncOpenAI with asyncio.gather for parallel pipeline calls.

rule.statement: Use AsyncOpenAI for any pipeline that spawns more than 2 parallel LLM calls; parallel async calls count toward the same TPM budget — throttle with a semaphore for large batches.
rule.rationale: Sequential calls at Tier 1 (500 RPM) can saturate the rate limit; async gather distributes the same calls within the time window, not beyond it.

example.code:
import asyncio from openai import AsyncOpenAI
async_client = AsyncOpenAI()
async def parallel_completions(prompts: list[str]) -> list[str]: async def get_completion(prompt: str) -> str: response = await async_client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o-mini", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}] ) return response.choices[0].message.content
return await asyncio.gather(*[get_completion(p) for p in prompts])
results = asyncio.run(parallel_completions(["Hello", "World", "!"]))
